title_normalized: Staff Engineer & Product Owner for ELS
success_metrics: delivering secure updates for unsupported Linux ecosystems
automation issues not impacting downstream SLAs
implied_pain_points: Patch and vulnerability automation needs stronger ownership and product direction.
Legacy systems need incremental refactoring without breaking downstream consumers.
The team needs someone who can improve reliability while balancing urgent security work with long-term technical health.

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV should prove end-to-end ownership of a production automation platform, including architecture decisions, delivery, and operational reliability. It should also show direct stakeholder coordination and the ability to turn ambiguous security and business requirements into a roadmap that ships.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Owning a Linux backporting and vulnerability pipeline at vendor-security scale
Formal people management with direct reports if it is not explicitly documented
Budget or P&L ownership
Deep GitLab runner operations if not shown in prior work
Daily production Python and Go coding if not proven on the CV
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps well because it shows ownership of an enterprise AI workflow platform with plugins, RAG, guardrails, evaluation, and cross-functional product decisions.
Lantern maps because multi-provider routing and circuit breaker patterns show resilience thinking that fits automation pipelines.
Lead Engineer work in CQRS and event-sourcing maps to architectural rigor, dependency management, and complex backend delivery.

success_metrics: performing to the expectations agreed with the team
implied_pain_points: The client needs a lead who can bridge frontend, backend, and generative AI work in one delivery stream.
The team needs someone who can ship safely, maintain product quality, and handle difficult issues in a production-grade web app.
The role needs strong technical leadership without losing focus on UI/UX and product details.

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV should prove hands-on delivery across frontend and backend, plus concrete LLM features shipped into production with quality controls. It should show shipping to real users, strong product judgment, and examples of safely deploying and iterating on AI-driven functionality.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Expert-level Next.js and React product engineering if his strongest evidence is platform or backend work
Hands-on UI/UX design ownership without design artifacts or shipped examples
A/B testing and safe-deployment leadership if not documented
Chatbot-first consumer product ownership if it is not shown in previous projects
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the strongest match because it proves shipping AI features into a real product with evaluation and guardrails.
Lantern maps because it shows LLM gateway and multi-provider routing experience, which is useful for AI-driven product work.
Technical Lead experience at Seven.One maps because it shows lead-level ownership and daily collaboration across a product team.

success_metrics: simple first, scalable where it matters
implied_pain_points: The team needs stronger architecture and delivery discipline across frontend and backend.
Production systems need hardening for reliability, observability, security, and performance.
The role is expected to unblock the team and keep scope pragmatic while improving deployment speed.

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV should prove ownership of a production stack across API design, CI/CD, containerized deployment, and operational monitoring. It should also show pragmatic architecture decisions, incident handling, and measurable improvements in reliability, performance, and delivery speed.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Production .NET ownership if that stack is not on his CV
AWS EKS or ECS operations if he has not run those environments directly
OpenAPI 3 ownership without a documented API design role
Deep PostgreSQL and Redis production experience if not shown
Front-end leadership in React, TypeScript, and MUI if not proven
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps because it demonstrates production platform ownership, evaluation, guardrails, and operational thinking.
Lantern maps because multi-provider routing and circuit breakers are a strong fit for resilience, fallback handling, and pragmatic system design.
The microservices and RabbitMQ background maps to queues, async patterns, and delivery in production systems.

success_metrics: strong product impact on thousands of learners
implied_pain_points: translate broad requirements into concrete technical designs
keep AI-first SaaS products scalable and resilient
protect long-term stability while shipping critical features
improve product performance and proactively address issues

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 4+ years on actual SaaS products; 2+ years as the primary technical contributor for a SaaS product

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A top CV needs to show end-to-end ownership of shipped SaaS features, with clear architecture decisions that improved scalability and resilience. It should also prove practical use of generative AI in delivery, not just interest in it, plus measurable product impact.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Deep consumer edtech domain ownership if not already evidenced
Hands-on frontend specialization if not documented
Daily generative AI usage in development workflows if not explicitly shown
Repeated sole ownership of SaaS product architecture at product scale if not supported
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps strongly to the AI-first SaaS/product ownership focus because it is an enterprise AI workflow platform with plugins, users, RAG, evaluation, and guardrails.
Lantern maps to resilient AI platform design because multi-provider routing and circuit breakers show reliability-oriented architecture.
Technical Lead experience at Seven.One maps to the ownership and technical trajectory parts of the role.

title_normalized: Senior Full Stack Product Software Engineer

architecture.scale_signals: millions of global users every day

hard_skills_must_have: backend storage systems such as MySQL, MyRocks, Memcached
success_metrics: serve millions of people every day
improve the interaction experience with generative AI models and the bot ecosystem
launch projects from ideation to implementation and launch
implied_pain_points: ship interactive AI-powered consumer experiences quickly
improve bot discovery and core messaging
coordinate across design, product, data science, QA, and engineering
balance feature velocity with sustainable code quality

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 5+ years of full-time professional full-stack experience building user-facing consumer web products; 3+ years with React, TypeScript, and GraphQL; 3+ years in Python or similar programming language

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to show consumer-facing full-stack delivery, especially on products with high user engagement and frequent iteration. It should prove React, TypeScript, GraphQL, and Python depth, plus evidence of leading or coaching engineers without losing execution speed.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Deep React, TypeScript, and GraphQL production expertise if not explicitly shown
Consumer web product ownership at million-user scale if not documented
Formal people management or direct reports if not evidenced
Strong front-end product engineering breadth beyond the provided profile
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps to the AI product-building side because it is a user-facing enterprise AI workflow platform with real adoption and multiple features shipped.
Lantern maps to the bot/model ecosystem complexity because it handles multi-provider routing and resilience in AI requests.
Technical Lead experience maps to the cross-functional coordination and end-to-end project ownership, but the CV should not overstate React/GraphQL depth unless separately evidenced.

success_metrics: stability, efficiency, and ease of use for ML engineers and data scientists
high-quality training and inference data availability
implied_pain_points: build a reliable ML and LLM platform for production use
improve deployment automation for models and platform components
keep serving APIs low-latency and highly available
support data science and engineering teams with usable internal tooling

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A top CV needs to show production platform ownership, not just model experimentation. It should demonstrate scalable API work, reliable CI/CD, cloud deployment, and concrete examples of operating ML or LLM systems with low latency and strong developer usability.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Hands-on AWS and serverless deployment ownership if not documented
LLM fine-tuning and production serving ownership if not explicitly shown
PySpark data pipeline implementation if not evidenced
Direct ML platform engineering depth beyond AI platform orchestration and application-layer work
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps well to ML platform reliability because it includes evaluation, guardrails, semantic caching, and an enterprise deployment surface.
Lantern maps to production serving resilience because multi-provider routing and circuit breakers align with robust model-serving concerns.
Prior microservices, CQRS/event-sourcing, and real-time systems experience map to the low-latency and high-availability parts of the role.

implied_pain_points: launch a new product initiative for mid-market customers
lead and mentor a high-performing engineering team
deliver scalable software across multiple concurrent projects
reduce blockers and technical risk
translate business needs into technical execution
shape engineering practices and quality standards

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to show hands-on leadership over shipping product software, not just people management. It should prove you can drive delivery across multiple concurrent initiatives, keep quality high, and communicate clearly with cross-functional and senior stakeholders. Evidence in Python, React-adjacent product work, and relational/NoSQL systems matters more than generic leadership language.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: formal hiring ownership and performance-review responsibility without explicit evidence
budget or P&L ownership
React-heavy frontend leadership if the CV does not document it
big-data platform scale beyond the documented 2,000-user AI workflow platform
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Technical Lead at Seven.One Entertainment Group: strongest match for hands-on leadership, cross-functional delivery, and shaping engineering practices.
Commander-4: best proof of shipping a scalable product platform with quality controls, evaluation, and guardrails under real user load.
Lantern LLM gateway and prior microservices/CQRS work: supports claims around architecture, reliability, and system design.

architecture.scale_signals: approximately 150 QA and Automation QA engineers across 10 global product teams
architecture.latency_reliability: release stability, predictability, and performance; reliable, scalable, high-performing product experiences

implied_pain_points: inconsistent quality practices across global product teams
need to modernize QA capabilities
need to adopt AI-enabled testing
need for clearer quality governance and standards
need to improve release stability and risk visibility

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV would need to show platform-wide quality leadership, not just feature-level testing. It should evidence ownership of QA strategy, automation, and release-risk reduction across distributed teams, plus the ability to brief executives with clear metrics and recommendations. The best match would also show experience applying AI to testing, evaluation, or quality workflows.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: direct ownership of a 150-person QA organization
career-long QA management or test automation leadership
formal responsibility for quality budgets or P&L
proven C-level QA reporting cadence
enterprise QA transformation ownership across 10 product teams
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4's evaluation, guardrails, and workflow reliability work maps to AI-enabled testing and quality metrics.
Lantern's multi-provider routing and circuit-breaker design maps to reliability and release stability.
Technical Lead leadership at Seven.One shows cross-functional platform ownership, but not dedicated QA management.

title_normalized: Senior AI Engineer - AI Patch Backporting
success_metrics: benchmark results stable and comparable over time
secure updates across multiple Linux distributions
implied_pain_points: patch backporting workflow has bottlenecks and weak points
experiments need a cleaner path into production
benchmarking is not yet stable or comparable over time
local dev and test harnesses need improvement
cross-component integration needs to be wired into the broader automation stack

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to prove ownership of complex AI systems in production, especially Python-based tooling that connects experimentation to CI/CD and operational workflows. It should show you can design abstractions, build benchmarking and evaluation systems, and ship improvements that make secure updates faster and more reliable across real environments. Evidence of LLM integration, multi-component architecture, and Linux-adjacent automation would be the most relevant.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: direct ownership of Linux patch backporting for unsupported distributions
deep Python-heavy automation experience in this exact domain
building benchmark datasets for patch backporting from scratch
hands-on PostgreSQL and MongoDB ownership in a security patching platform
shipping code-backporting agents at production scale
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps to LLM workflow orchestration, RAG, evaluation, and guardrails.
Lantern maps to multi-provider routing and production AI infrastructure patterns.
Lead Engineer and microservices/event-sourcing work map to ownership of complex components and system integration.

architecture.scale_signals: across departments, marketing, operations, HR, and more

hard_skills_nice_to_have: training internal employees on tools and workflows
success_metrics: how many employees confidently integrate AI into their daily tasks
how well those solutions solve real problems
implied_pain_points: AI usage needs to be democratized across the organization
employees need structured enablement to adopt AI tools in daily work
department-specific pain points require tailored training
training content must keep pace with rapid AI developments
the organization needs a network of AI champions

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV should show you can turn complex AI capabilities into understandable training and adoption programs for non-technical audiences. It needs evidence of workshop delivery, curriculum design, and translating tools like generative AI and automation platforms into measurable workflow improvements. The best proof is not just AI familiarity, but repeated success getting people to actually use the tools.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: formal L&D or instructional design leadership
company-wide training ownership across business functions
change-management program leadership for AI adoption
hands-on facilitation of large workshop series as a primary role
building a department-wide AI champions network
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps to internal AI enablement because it is a concrete enterprise AI workflow platform.
Lantern maps to explaining and operationalizing AI tooling for stakeholders because it sits between users and multiple model providers.
Technical Lead experience shows cross-functional communication, but not dedicated training or L&D ownership.

architecture.latency_reliability: fault-tolerant, highly scalable; strong focus on performance and reliability

success_metrics: develop, test, deploy and monitor your changes end to end
top-tier coding quality through best practices, rigorous testing, logging, and metrics
enhance product architecture, knowledge models, user experience, performance, and reliability
reduce security operations complexity and cost
implied_pain_points: build globally distributed, fault-tolerant cloud ecosystems at massive event volume
advance Charlotte AI capabilities with LLMs and custom model work
improve backend microservices, data pipelines, architecture, and reliability

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to prove hands-on ownership of distributed systems, production AI features, and reliability at scale. It should show end-to-end delivery across build, test, deploy, and monitor, plus concrete examples of LLM integration and data-pipeline work.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: deep security-industry operating experience
shipped Go-based production systems if not explicitly true
trained custom LLMs from scratch at large scale
owned systems handling 3 trillion events per day
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the closest match because it shows enterprise AI workflow delivery, RAG, evaluation, guardrails, and end-to-end product ownership.
Lantern maps well to production reliability and model-routing concerns because it includes multi-provider routing and circuit breaker behavior.
The Technical Lead and prior microservices/event-sourcing work map to the distributed systems and backend engineering parts of the role.

success_metrics: identify critical defects early in the development cycle
deliver actionable recommendations for ongoing improvement
implied_pain_points: slow release cycles
error-prone software
defects discovered after release
outdated testing approaches
need for AI-driven QA automation

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 2+ years in a QA automation role

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to show direct QA automation ownership, Selenium usage, and AI-assisted testing workflows. The most persuasive evidence is measurable impact on release speed, defect leakage, and test coverage quality.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: being a pure QA automation specialist with deep Selenium ownership
building AI-driven QA pipelines end to end if not actually done
leading manual test execution programs
claiming CI/CD quality engineering leadership without direct QA evidence
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the closest analog because its evaluation, guardrails, and workflow automation show disciplined validation loops, though it is not a direct QA background.
Lantern adds reliability patterns such as routing and circuit-breaking, which can support a quality-engineering narrative around resilience.
The candidate still lacks direct Selenium-first QA experience, so the fit is partial rather than strong.

success_metrics: significant impact on the partner organization's competitiveness
define success metrics and KPI frameworks
implied_pain_points: need to define strategy for complex, high-impact data products
need to align multiple teams around a multi-year roadmap
need to translate complex data concepts into business impact for executives
need to drive organizational change around data technologies

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 10+ years in product management or data analytics; 5+ years focused on data products or analytics platforms

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to prove strategic ownership of data or analytics products, KPI design, and cross-functional alignment with executives. It should show that the candidate can turn ambiguous business needs into a roadmap, define measurable outcomes, and communicate clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: being a seasoned product manager with 10+ years in PM or data analytics unless that is explicitly true
deep daily SQL and analytics leadership if the candidate has not done it
executive data-product ownership with multi-year roadmap accountability unless evidenced
direct people management of product managers
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the strongest match because it shows platform thinking, cross-functional delivery, evaluation discipline, and ownership of a complex AI product.
Lantern maps to data/product architecture decisions because it demonstrates multi-provider routing and reliability-oriented platform design.
The Technical Lead role at Seven.One is relevant for enterprise collaboration and end-to-end delivery, but it is still an engineering-led rather than product-management background.

architecture.scale_signals: 35+ million users in 2 years; target 100M first, 500M next

success_metrics: help build and scale the platform
manage, monitor and scale existing services
provision infrastructure and platform services through Infrastructure-as-Code
run experiments and bring new ideas
implied_pain_points: rapidly expanding user base and tech team
need infrastructure support for engineers
need to scale existing services reliably
need infrastructure provisioning through IaC
need technical solutioning for a fast-moving product roadmap

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 5+ years in public cloud, Kubernetes, GitOps, Infrastructure-as-Code

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV should show hands-on ownership of production infrastructure for a fast-growing platform, including IaC-based provisioning, observability, and operational scaling. It should also show close collaboration with product and engineering teams, plus the ability to read application code across Go, Python, and shell to support delivery and debugging.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Direct Kubernetes, GitOps, or Terraform ownership unless explicitly shown on the CV
Public cloud platform administration by name
Operating at 35M-user infrastructure scale unless backed by measurable evidence
English fluency unless explicitly stated
Hiring, performance management, or budget ownership
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps well because it shows ownership of a production AI platform with 42 plugins, 2,000 users, RAG, semantic caching, evaluation, and guardrails, which is strong evidence for scaling and operating a platform.
Lantern maps well because multi-provider routing and circuit breaker work is directly relevant to service reliability, platform resilience, and operational engineering.

The Seven.One Entertainment Group technical lead role and earlier microservices/RabbitMQ and CQRS/event-sourcing work are the best evidence for cross-functional engineering support and distributed-systems thinking.
